markdown_projection.note: JSON contract is authority. No markdown projection is generated for WP-KERNEL-009 (prevents projection drift).

scope.scope: Represent new model/parser extractions as probationary until thresholds, evidence, and conflict checks pass. This MT belongs to MemoryGraphAndClaims. Implement the Handshake-native memory system translated from research into product contracts.
constraints: Use the existing microtask_plan_draft execution surface only; do not introduce a separate MT execution surface or suite field.

Core Handshake behavior must be Handshake-native: no outside app, outside server, external daemon, Docker-only path, SQLite fallback, Redis/Valkey cache authority, or sidecar paper as proof or authority.

Allowed external runtime inputs are limited to operator-provided models, GGUF/model files, tensors/safetensors, and CUI portable artifacts where the owning WP explicitly gates them; Tiptap and Monaco are allowed only as bundled/lockfile-governed product libraries integrated into Handshake.

PostgreSQL plus EventLedger is canonical for durable state, receipts, indexing evidence, runtime proof, and validation; generated markdown, wiki pages, HTML, context bundles, debug reports, and UI projections are projections only.
Every implementation handoff must be restartable by a no-context local model using only the WP/MT contract, current Master Spec bundle, product code, tests, and receipts.
Paper-derived memory architecture must be expressed as Handshake-native MemoryOntology, MemoryFact, MemoryPassage, ClaimLifecycle, ConflictResolution, and EvidenceSpan contracts.
Probationary claims cannot become stable retrieval facts without evidence, conflict checks, and EventLedger promotion.
scope.expected_behavior: ProbationaryExtractionLifecycle leaves a concrete, restartable implementation or activation artifact with PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority, Master Spec anchors, UserManual impact, and validator-readable evidence.
acceptance_criteria: ProbationaryExtractionLifecycle is implemented or specified with enough detail for a no-context local model to continue from the MT alone.
The MT names exact product/governance files, state tables or APIs, constraints, failure modes, and verification evidence.
The MT rejects sidecar research-paper authority and outside-runtime proof; accepted research ideas are restated as Handshake-native requirements.
The MT handoff names HBR rows proven, blocked, or not applicable with concrete reasons.
verification: Parse the WP/MT contract and confirm this MT keeps all required fields populated.
Run the smallest relevant product, governance, or fixture test available for the touched surface; if not implemented yet, define the exact future test and negative case.
For UI/editor/UserManual surfaces, use visual debugging or declare why no GUI surface exists for this MT.
Confirm no SQLite, Docker-default, external-app, external-daemon, sidecar-paper, or mock-only proof satisfies the MT.
handoff_requirements: List files changed or intentionally left untouched.
List commands/tests run and exact pass/fail/blocker status.
List EventLedger/PostgreSQL evidence, receipt IDs, fixtures, visual-debug artifacts, and repair queues produced or expected.
List remaining risks, compatibility shims, deprecated wording, and next MTs unblocked.

evidence: Built+verified 98beb6d7; MemoryGraphAndClaims group: 21 memory-graph tests pass on real PostgreSQL (ontology/facts/conflict/bridge/projection/api/fixtures); migrations 0240-0243 with down+rollback test; facts back knowledge_claims (reuse lifecycle+conflict authority), projections marked authority_class=projection.

wp_validator_review_v2.review_method: Integration Validator multi-agent workflow (wf_0ac25815-485 + wf_98386044-70a): independent per-MT reviewer + adversarial confirm on FAIL. No ACP. Governance/repo paperwork non-blocking per operator directive (2026-06-18). Bar = good code vs MT contract + WP packet + current Master Spec v02.194, plus the WP_VALIDATOR Spec-Realism Gate.
wp_validator_review_v2.summary: MT-119 (ProbationaryExtractionLifecycle) requires that new model/parser extractions stay probationary until evidence thresholds, conflict checks, and an EventLedger promotion receipt pass. The code implements this in real Handshake-native PostgreSQL with both app-side and DB-trigger enforcement, and proves it end-to-end (positive + negative paths) against the managed PostgreSQL cluster.
findings: storage/knowledge_memory.rs:240-286 upsert_memory_ontology_term creates terms with lifecycle_state='probationary' (DB DEFAULT) and increments observation_count on re-derivation without moving lifecycle - faithfully models the probationary accrual the MT requires.

storage/knowledge_memory.rs:333-367 promote_memory_ontology_term enforces the promotion gate app-side: rejects non-probationary (Conflict), rejects unpromotable (Validation), and requires a promotion_receipt_event_id - probationary cannot become stable without evidence/approval + receipt, exactly per MT constraint 'Probationary claims cannot become stable retrieval facts without evidence, conflict checks, and EventLedger promotion.'

storage/knowledge_memory.rs:178-182 is_promotable() encodes the threshold-OR-operator-approval gate (observation_count >= promotion_threshold || operator_approved), matching the translated spec promotion rule.

migrations/0240_knowledge_memory_ontology.sql:145-160 stable_requires_receipt trigger and :165-192 transition_guard trigger provide defense-in-depth at the DB layer: stable rows MUST carry a promotion_receipt_event_id (FK to real kernel_event_ledger, line 78-79), and lifecycle transitions are constrained to probationary->stable|retired, stable->retired, retired terminal. Raw-SQL bypass is blocked.

tests/knowledge_memory_ontology_tests.rs exercises real proof: ontology_term_probationary_then_promoted_with_receipt (positive, appends a real kernel_event_ledger receipt via pg.db.append_kernel_event), under_threshold_term_cannot_be_promoted (negative app-side + raw-SQL stable refusal), ontology_lifecycle_guard_blocks_illegal_transitions (stable->probationary and retired resurrection refused at DB).

tests/knowledge_pg_support.rs:1-25 confirms PostgreSQL-only proof: resolution order POSTGRES_TEST_URL -> DATABASE_URL -> Handshake-managed cluster (managed_postgres::ensure_running, no Docker/daemon); NO SQLite/in-memory/mock fallback - binaries-absent returns None (SKIP), every other failure panics so a broken cluster cannot look green. Satisfies sub-rule 2 real-resource-touch.

Spec alignment: Master Spec v02.194 section 2.3.13.11 (lines 2696-2715) mandates PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority, claim/memory lifecycle discipline, and forbids SQLite/markdown-vault authority - the implementation conforms.

Sub-rule 1: grep of the MT-119 surfaces found no todo!/unimplemented!/unreachable!/Unavailable/'deferred'/'pending MT-' escapes; only hit is knowledge_memory/fixtures.rs:8 comment explicitly stating fixtures do NOT mock or stub.

Aside (non-blocking, governance-only): MT contract lifecycle.claimed_by/completed_by are null and the commit is co-authored; per the relaxed bar this is expected paperwork state, not a code defect, and sub_rule_3 is NA.
evidence_checked: wtc-kernel-009 commit 98beb6d7 (git show --stat, single commit covering MT-113/119/120)
grep for deferred-live escapes across MT-119 surfaces

minimum_controls: No placeholders, mocks, scaffolding, or deferred-live escapes; runtime proof against Handshake-managed PostgreSQL/EventLedger where the contract names durable state.
